info.name: Berkeley-testing
info.description:
Test suite for the Berkeley Payments **Card Issuing** API (staging).

Base URLs:
- Staging: https://api.staging.pungle.co
- Production: https://api.pungle.io

Auth: collection-level Bearer token using `{{api_key}}`.

## Variable strategy
- **Collection variable** `card_issuing_path` (= `api/v1/card_issuing`) — stable API structure.
- **Environment** (Berkeley-Staging) holds everything instance-specific: `base_url`, `api_key`, `program_id`, and the chained values `cardholder_id`, `processor_reference`, `account_id`, `last_four_digits`, `value_load_id`.

## Run order (for a full chained Collection Runner / Newman run)
1. **Cardholders → Create Cardholder** sets `cardholder_id` + `processor_reference`.
2. **Accounts and Cards → Get Account By Processor Reference** resolves `account_id`; **Get Account Details** sets `last_four_digits`.
3. **Value Loads** use `account_id`; **Create Value Load** sets `value_load_id`.
4. **Programs** is independent and can run any time.

Conditional endpoints (Activate Card, Retrieve PIN, Value Unload) assert leniently because they depend on program configuration (physical vs virtual, Canadian, unload-enabled) and log the reason when not applicable.

A collection-level test asserts no 5xx on every request.

item.name: Cardholders
item.description: CRUD coverage for the Cardholders resource. `Create Cardholder` seeds the chained variables (`cardholder_id`, `processor_reference`) used by the Accounts & Cards and Value Loads folders, so run it first.

request.description:
Creates a new Cardholder + primary Account. Seeds `cardholder_id` and `processor_reference` into the active environment for downstream requests.

The pre-request script randomises `email` and `external_tag` so the request is safely re-runnable.

Required: program_id, first_name, last_name, email, city, state, postal_code, country.

exec: // Make the request idempotent-friendly across re-runs
pm.test("Cardholder id is returned", function () { pm.expect(data).to.have.property("id"); });

request.description: Returns full cardholder profile, accounts and cards. Best-effort extraction of `last_four_digits` for card-level requests (authoritative account_id is set by 'Get Account By Processor Reference').

exec: pm.test("Returned id matches requested cardholder_id", function () {
// best-effort: pull a card's last four for card-level endpoints

request.description: Lists all cardholders. Pagination via limit/offset (disabled by default).

Partial update of cardholder personal info. Send only fields being changed.

KNOWN BEHAVIOUR (documented test case): updating address fields too soon after a prior address change returns `cannot_update_resource` ("Cardholder address was updated recently"). Address fields are intentionally omitted here to keep the happy path green; see the negative folder for the cooldown assertion.

Note: `emboss_line2` is not yet supported on update.
Negative test: omits required fields (program_id, first_name, email...). Expect a 4xx validation error, never a 2xx.

exec: pm.test("Rejected with a client error (4xx)", function () { pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.be.within(400, 499); });
pm.test("Did not create a cardholder", function () { pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.not.be.oneOf([200, 201]); });

request.description: Negative test: fetch a cardholder id that should not exist. Expect 404 (or 4xx).

exec: pm.test("Rejected with a client error (4xx)", function () { pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.be.within(400, 499); });

item.name: Accounts and Cards
item.description: Account- and card-level operations. 'Get Account By Processor Reference' resolves `processor_reference` (from Create Cardholder) into the numeric `account_id` used by every other request here and by Value Loads. Run it first within this folder.

request.description: Resolves an Account by its processor reference (proxy number). Sets the chained `account_id`.

exec: if (data.id) { pm.environment.set("account_id", data.id); console.log("Saved account_id =", data.id); }

query.description: Include current-day value loads with authorizations

request.description: Combined account dashboard: status, balance, cards, authorizations, transactions. Extracts a card's `last_four_digits` for card-level endpoints.

exec: pm.test("status_code is a known status", function () {

request.description: Returns settled_balance, available_balance and (deprecated) balance as strings.

exec: pm.test("available_balance is a numeric string", function () {

request.description: Lists pending/authorized transactions for the account.
Settled transactions, paginated. NOTE: some program configs do not return a page count and instead surface a `list_exhausted` error at the end of the list.

exec: pm.test("Transactions data is an array", function () {

request.description: Physical card order/shipping status. May be empty for virtual programs.

Activates a PHYSICAL card by Account ID + last four (Sample 1). Physical cards only — virtual cards must use 'Modify Account Status' with mark_card_active.

Update expiry_month/expiry_year/cvv to match the real card under test. Lenient assertion: success on 201/210, otherwise logs the precondition reason.

exec: console.log("Activate Card not applicable (virtual card / bad expiry / already active). Code:", pm.response.code);

request.description:
Changes card/account status. Body `status` accepts: mark_card_active, mark_card_lost, mark_card_stolen, initiate_card_lost, initiate_card_stolen, suspend, unsuspend.

EDGE CASE worth testing: `lost`/`stolen` are terminal — a card in those states cannot be moved back. Suspend/unsuspend is the safe reversible pair for staging.

exec: console.log("Modify status returned", pm.response.code, "- check last_four_digits / current status.");

request.description: Retrieves the card PIN. CANADIAN PROGRAMS ONLY — expect a 4xx on non-CA programs. Lenient assertion accordingly.

exec: console.log("Retrieve PIN unavailable (Canadian programs only). Code:", pm.response.code);

request.description: Negative test: balance for a non-existent account. Expect 4xx.

exec: pm.test("Rejected with a client error (4xx)", function () { pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.be.within(400, 499); });

item.name: Value Loads
item.description: Load / unload funds against an account and read back value-load records. Depends on `account_id` (set by the Accounts & Cards folder). 'Create Value Load' seeds `value_load_id`.

request.description: Loads funds (amount in minor units; 100 = $1.00) onto an account from the master funding account. Reloadable cards only. Pre-request randomises external_tag + idempotency_key for safe re-runs; seeds `value_load_id`.

exec: pm.test("Value load id returned", function () { pm.expect(data).to.have.property("id"); });
if (data.id) { pm.environment.set("value_load_id", data.id); console.log("Saved value_load_id =", data.id); }

query.description: Program the value load was created under

request.description: Lists value loads, filterable by program_id / external_tag with limit & offset.

exec: pm.test("Has count and data array", function () {

request.description: Fetches a single value-load record by id.
Unloads funds from an account. MUST BE ENABLED PER PROGRAM — expect a 4xx if not enabled on staging. Lenient assertion accordingly.

exec: console.log("Value Unload not enabled for this program (per docs). Code:", pm.response.code);

request.description: Negative test: load against a non-existent account. Expect 4xx.

exec: pm.test("Rejected with a client error (4xx)", function () { pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.be.within(400, 499); });

item.name: Programs
item.description: Program-level reads. Independent of the other folders (uses `program_id` directly). Also hosts the auth negative test.

request.description: Returns program name, type, status, currency and master funding account number.

exec: pm.test("Program status is active", function () { pm.expect(data.status).to.eql("active"); });

request.description: Master funding account balance for the program.
Negative test: a non-existent program id. Expect 4xx.

exec: pm.test("Rejected with a client error (4xx)", function () { pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.be.within(400, 499); });

request.description: Negative/security test: overrides the collection bearer token with an invalid one. Expect 401 (or 403).

exec: // Universal guard applied to every request in the collection
